name: p0-sub-board-tripping-after-rain
description: Our DB sub-board keeps tripping the main earth leakage after it rains. Has happened three times in the last week, only after rain. The board has several circuits — geyser, plug points, lights, pool pump. No photo attached.

hypotheses.label: Water ingress at an exterior plug or light fitting
evidence_for: Tripping correlates with rain — outdoor circuits are the typical entry point for moisture
Sub-board with mixed indoor/outdoor circuits commonly fails this way

hypotheses.label: Geyser element earth leakage
evidence_for: Element burnout is a common earth-leakage trip cause
evidence_against: Geyser leakage usually trips regardless of weather

hypotheses.label: Pool pump motor earth leakage
evidence_for: Pool pump motors are outdoor and weather-exposed
Damaged seals admit water into windings — symptom emerges after rain
evidence_against: Cannot confirm without isolating the pool circuit

mockAgentOutput.what_we_dont_know: Which specific circuit on the sub-board is responsible for the trip — the user has not yet isolated circuits one at a time.
mockAgentOutput.why_it_matters: Knowing the offending circuit narrows the fault from an open-ended 'electrical fault' to a specific component (geyser element, outdoor socket, pool pump motor, etc.) that the contractor can quote on directly.

chips.text: Trips only when the pool circuit is on.
Trips even with everything off except the geyser.
Trips only when outdoor lights or plugs are powered.

committed_observations: sub-board trips main earth-leakage after rain
multiple circuits on the board (geyser, plugs, lights, pool)
explicit_unknowns: which specific circuit is the leak source
whether one device or wiring is implicated

expected.must_ask_about: which specific circuit trips when the others are isolated

failure_baseline_today.observed_behaviour: System force-commits 'Electrical fault — sub-board tripping' with confidence 60-70 and no specific question. Contractor arrives without a hypothesis.
failure_baseline_today.why_it_fails: Without explicit per-facet uncertainty, the single confidence integer floats around the threshold and the system either commits to a useless generic diagnosis or asks a generic 'tell us more' question. There is no rubric for 'multiple plausible circuits, isolation question would resolve'.

expected.phase_6_ask_rationale: Top hypothesis confidence_alone=0.5 (<0.75) and gap to next=0.10 (<0.25). Discriminating chips targeting circuit isolation directly partition the hypothesis space — so ask, not commit_low_confidence.
